title: Rust frame write_to performs no field-consistency validation (Python/C enforce)
description: rust/lichen-link/src/frame.rs:259-283 writes whatever mic/dst_addr slices are given; nothing checks mic.len() == mic_length.mic_len() or dst_addr.len() == addr_mode.addr_len(). A mismatched frame serializes with an LLSec that doesn't describe the body and does not round-trip through Rust's own parser. Python frame.py:101-115 (_validate enforces both); C frame.c:131-136 (mic_len vs mic_length check, address length derived from mode). No current caller violates it (build_frame uses constant 4-byte MIC + derived addr mode) - robustness gap only. Fix: return an error on inconsistent fields.
